item.title: Adobe同意支付1.5亿美元以解决涉嫌违反《在线购物者保护法》的指控
item.description: 美国司法部周五表示，Adobe公司已同意支付1.5亿美元的和解金并接受禁令，以解决有关该软件公司订阅做法违反《恢复在线购物者信心法》的指控。【原文：The U.S. ​Justice Department ‌said on Friday that ​Adobe ​Inc has agreed ⁠to ​a $150 million ​settlement and an injunction to ​resolve ​allegations that the ‌software ⁠firm's subscription practices violated ​the ​Restore ⁠Online Shoppers' Confidence ​Act.】

item.title: 现代汽车因一起致命事故，对部分2026款Palisade SUV下达停售令
item.description: 现代汽车周五表示，由于电动座椅存在问题，且俄亥俄州发生了一起导致一名两岁女童死亡的事故，该公司已发布停售令，并将召回美国和加拿大部分2026款Palisade SUV。【原文：Hyundai Motor said on Friday it is ​issuing a stop sale ‌and will recall some 2026 Palisade SUVs in ​the United States ​and Canada over an issue ⁠with power seats ​following an incident in which ​a two-year-old girl was killed in Ohio.】

item.title: 随着春播临近，伊朗战争导致美国农民无法获得价格合理的化肥
item.description: 美国和加拿大的农民原本就对又将面临低利润甚至亏损的一年感到忧心忡忡，如今他们又因难以购得化肥而可能面临春播受阻的困境。自伊朗战争导致全球贸易陷入瘫痪以来，市场上现有化肥的价格已飙升逾三分之一。【原文：Farmers in the U.S. and Canada, who were already worried about prospects for another year ​of low profits or losses, now could have spring planting disrupted as they struggle to find fertilizer, and prices ‌for any available supplies have spiked more than a third since the war in Iran paralyzed global trade.】

item.title: 欧洲迈出禁止AI生成的儿童性虐待图像的第一步
item.description: 上周五，欧盟各国政府提议将这一条款纳入两年前通过的欧盟里程碑式人工智能法规，欧洲由此迈出了禁止生成儿童性虐待材料的人工智能实践的第一步。【原文：Europe on Friday took the first step towards outlawing artificial ​intelligence practices which generate child sexual ‌abuse material after EU governments proposed to add this provision to the bloc's landmark AI rules adopted ​two years ago.】

item.title: 独家：消息人士称，SpaceX聘请吉博森·邓恩律师事务所和戴维斯·波克·沃德威尔律师事务所作为其重磅IPO的法律顾问
item.description: 据两位知情人士透露，SpaceX及其承销此次重磅IPO的银行已选定两家律师事务所，为该交易提供法律咨询。这是企业在筹备上市时采取的首批具体举措之一。【原文：SpaceX and the banks underwriting its ​blockbuster IPO have chosen two law firms to provide legal advice on the deal, ‌one of the first concrete steps companies take when preparing a public listing, two people familiar with the plans said.】

item.title: 伊朗局势给炼油企业带来压力，加州油价大幅上涨
item.description: 由于若干特殊因素，加州的燃油成本涨幅甚至超过了美国其他地区；预计伊朗战争的连锁反应将使该州的汽油零售价升至每加仑10美元，而航空燃油价格在短短两周内已飙升47%。【原文：Fuel costs in California are soaring even more than in the rest of the U.S. due to several unique factors, with fallout from the war ​in Iran forecast to push pump prices in the state to $10 a gallon and jet fuel prices jumping 47% in just two weeks.】

item.title: 中国开发商富力地产公布了一项总额达47亿美元的全面债务重组计划
item.description: 深陷困境的中国房地产开发商富力地产于周五公布了其离岸债务重组的详细条款，其中包括发行新股、可转换债券及长期有担保票据的计划。【原文：Embattled Chinese property developer Fantasia on Friday unveiled detailed terms of its offshore debt restructuring, including plans to issue ​new equity, convertible bonds and long-dated secured notes.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道：随着AI编程工作受挫，马斯克再次罢免xAI创始人
item.description: 据《金融时报》周五报道，埃隆·马斯克在其人工智能公司xAI引发了一轮新的裁员潮，由于他对该公司编程部门表现不佳感到不满，又有更多联合创始人被扫地出门。【原文：Elon Musk has triggered a fresh wave of job cuts at his AI firm xAI, with more co-founders pushed out amid his dissatisfaction with ​the underperformance of the startup's coding division, the Financial Times reported on Friday.】

item.title: 亚马逊获胜：卢森堡法院撤销创纪录的8.54亿美元隐私罚款
item.description: 周五，亚马逊针对卢森堡隐私监管机构开出的创纪录7.46亿欧元（8.544亿美元）罚款提起的上诉获得胜诉。法院认定该监管机构未妥善开展分析工作，必须重新评估此案。【原文：Amazon on Friday won its appeal against a record 746-million-euro ($854.4 million) fine imposed by ​Luxembourg’s privacy regulator, after a court found the ‌watchdog had not properly carried out its analysis and must reassess the case.】

item.title: 美国烟草监管机构对电子烟持谨慎态度，但已批准Glas电子烟的许可证
item.description: 根据一份已获许可产品的公开清单，美国食品和药物管理局本周又批准了一个电子烟品牌上市销售，但仅限烟草口味。【原文：The U.S. Food ​and Drug Administration this week granted a licence for sale for one more vape brand, ‌but only in tobacco flavour, according to a public list of licensed products.】

item.title: 五角大楼负责人表示，他热切希望特朗普的盟友收购CNN，同时猛烈抨击该媒体的战争报道
item.description: 五角大楼负责人皮特·赫格塞斯周五表示，他迫切希望特朗普的盟友、派拉蒙-天舞影业首席执行官大卫·埃利森接管美国有线电视新闻网（CNN），同时他批评了美国新闻媒体对伊朗战争的报道。【原文：Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth on Friday said he was ​eager to see Trump ally and Paramount Skydance CEO David Ellison take over CNN, as he criticized ‌the U.S. news media's coverage of the Iran war.】

item.title: 据彭博社报道，波音公司正在维修25架存在布线缺陷的737 MAX客机
item.description: 据彭博社周五援引知情人士的话报道，波音公司正在维修多达25架存在布线缺陷的未交付737 MAX客机，并表示第一季度的交付可能面临延迟。【原文：Boeing ‌is repairing as many as 25 undelivered 737 MAX ​jets with wiring flaws ​and said first-quarter deliveries could ⁠face delays, Bloomberg News ​reported on Friday, citing ​people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 美国钢铁工人联合会（USW）成员投票否决了英国石油公司（BP）向惠廷炼油厂工人提出的最终方案
item.description: 美国钢铁工人联合会（USW）的成员于周四投票，以压倒性多数否决了能源巨头英国石油公司（BP）向其印第安纳州怀廷炼油厂员工提出的所谓“最后、最佳且最终”的合同提案。【原文：The U.S. United SteelWorkers (USW) union ​members voted on Thursday to overwhelmingly reject what energy giant BP ‌called its "last, best, and final" contract proposal to those working at its Whiting refinery in Indiana.】

item.title: 美国必需消费品股光彩褪色，高估值与黯淡的盈利前景形成鲜明对比
item.description: 分析师表示，在美国必需消费品股今年强劲上涨之后，随着盈利前景转暗，投资者开始质疑其高估值，该类股票正逐渐失宠。【原文：After a strong rally this year, U.S. consumer staples stocks are falling out of favor, with investors starting to question the high valuation as earnings prospects dim, analysts said.】

item.title: 巴克莱预计美联储将因通胀担忧推迟降息
item.description: 巴克莱银行周五效仿高盛，将美国联邦储备委员会今年首次降息的预期时间从6月推迟至9月，理由是中东冲突加剧了通胀风险。【原文：Barclays on Friday joined Goldman Sachs in delaying its forecast for the ​U.S. Federal Reserve's first interest rate cut this year to ‌September from June, citing heightened risks to inflation from the conflict in the Middle East.】

item.title: 史上最严重的全球石油供应中断
item.description: 国际能源署表示，霍尔木兹海峡的封锁引发了全球石油市场历史上最严重的供应中断，预计3月份石油供应量将减少约800万桶/日，降幅约为8%。【原文：The International Energy Agency said the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has triggered the largest disruption to global oil markets in history, with supply expected to fall by about 8 million barrels per day in March, or around 8%.】

item.title: Cerebras Systems 与亚马逊达成协议，将在亚马逊云平台上提供 Cerebras 人工智能芯片
item.description: 亚马逊（Amazon.com）和Cerebras Systems周五表示，双方已达成协议，将把两家公司的计算芯片整合到一项新服务中，旨在加速聊天机器人、编程工具及其他人工智能服务。【原文：Amazon.com and Cerebras Systems on Friday said they have reached a deal to combine the two companies' computing chips in a new service aimed at speeding ​up chatbots, coding tools and other artificial intelligence services.】

item.title: 1月份美国职位空缺增加，但招聘势头疲软
item.description: 1月份美国职位空缺有所增加，但招聘情况平淡，这与劳动力市场保持稳定相符。【原文：U.S. job openings increased in January, but hiring was lackluster, consistent with ​a stable labor market.】

item.title: 受中东战争影响，3月初美国消费者信心指数下滑
item.description: 周五公布的一项调查显示，受中东战争推高汽油价格以及家庭对个人财务状况的担忧影响，3月初美国消费者信心有所下滑。【原文：U.S. consumer sentiment ebbed in early March as war in the Middle East raised ​gasoline prices and households worried about personal finances, ‌a survey showed on Friday.】

item.title: 二手车零售商Carvana宣布首次股票拆分
item.description: 二手车零售商Carvana周五宣布，将进行首次股票拆分，每股拆分为五股，受此消息提振，其股价上涨2.8%。【原文：Used-car retailer Carvana said on Friday it would split each of its shares ​into five in its first stock ‌split, sending its shares up 2.8%.】

item.title: 瑞士部长表示，瑞士将坚持与美国进行贸易谈判的既定方针
item.description: 瑞士经济部长盖伊·帕尔梅林周五表示，瑞士将继续遵循与美国进行贸易谈判的现有授权，旨在就去年达成的关税协议敲定最终方案。【原文：Switzerland is sticking to its existing mandate for trade negotiations with the ​United States that aim to finalise ‌a deal over tariffs struck last year, Economy Minister Guy Parmelin said on Friday.】

item.title: 受伊朗局势影响，迪拜加密货币活动取消
item.description: 主办方周五表示，原定于4月底在迪拜举行的一场大型加密货币会议已取消，理由是“该地区局势不明朗”。【原文：A major Dubai crypto conference scheduled for the end of April has been cancelled, ​the organisers said on Friday, citing "uncertainty in the region".】

item.title: 伊朗发动石油冲击以削弱美国军事实力
item.description: 三位熟悉伊朗计划的地区消息人士称，早在美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击之前，伊朗伊斯兰共和国就已经制定了自己的应对策略：将全球主要的石油生命线作为筹码，以此来抵消敌方的军事优势。【原文：Long before the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran, the Islamic Republic had devised its own weapon: holding the world’s main oil lifeline hostage to offset its foes' military superiority, three regional sources familiar with Iranian ​planning said.】

item.title: 美国1月份核心资本 goods 订单意外持平
item.description: 1月份，美国主要资本 goods 的新订单意外持平，而这些产品的出货量则出现下滑，这表明第一季度初企业的设备支出疲软。【原文：New orders for key U.S.-manufactured capital goods were unexpectedly unchanged in January and shipments of these products fell, pointing to weakness ​in business spending on equipment early in the first quarter.】

item.title: 查看 PCE 通胀报告符合预期，GDP 表现疲软；市场走高
item.description: 美国商务部周五表示，1月份个人消费支出价格指数（PCE）的涨幅基本符合预期，这是通胀在上月与伊朗爆发战争之前仍相对温和的最新迹象。【原文：The U.S. Commerce Department said on Friday its Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index (PCE) rose largely in line with expectations in January, the latest sign that inflation remained relatively well ​behaved ahead of the war with Iran that began last month.】

item.title: Ulta Beauty股价下跌，因成本上升挤压利润率；新任首席执行官将重点推进TikTok业务
item.description: 周五盘前交易中，Ulta Beauty股价下跌约8%，原因是成本上涨盖过了强劲的需求；不过，分析师对其在新任首席执行官领导下、以TikTok为首的数字化战略表示乐观，认为该战略将吸引年轻且富裕的消费者。【原文：Ulta Beauty's shares fell about 8% in premarket trading on Friday as rising costs overshadowed strong demand, although analysts expressed optimism about its TikTok-led ​digital push under a new CEO to attract younger and affluent shoppers.】

item.title: 美国第四季度GDP增长率下调至0.7%
item.description: 美国政府周五公布的数据显示，受消费者支出和企业投资数据下调影响，第四季度美国经济增长放缓幅度比最初预期的更为明显。【原文：U.S. economic growth slowed more sharply than initially thought in the fourth quarter ​amid downward revisions to consumer spending and ‌business investment, government data showed on Friday.】

item.title: Adobe将支付7500万美元以解决美国针对其收费及订阅取消问题的诉讼
item.description: Adobe周五表示，将向美国政府支付7500万美元，以解决一起指控其损害消费者权益的诉讼。该诉讼称，这家Photoshop和Acrobat的制造商在其最受欢迎的订阅计划中隐瞒了高额解约费，并故意设置障碍，使消费者难以取消订阅。【原文：Adobe said on Friday ​it will pay $75 ‌million to the U.S. government ​to resolve ​a lawsuit accusing the ⁠Photoshop and ​Acrobat maker of ​harming consumers by concealing hefty termination fees ​in its ​most popular subscription plan, ‌and ⁠making it difficult to cancel subscriptions.】

item.title: 交易员押注美联储将在9月前降息
item.description: 交易员们周五押注，美联储很可能将于9月再次降息，此前政府数据显示，按美联储目标指标衡量的通胀水平并未如年初所担忧的那样高涨。【原文：The Federal Reserve will probably next cut interest ​rates in September, traders bet on Friday ‌after government data showed inflation by the Fed's targeted measure wasn't quite as hot as feared at the ​start of the year.】

item.title: 1月份美国消费者支出和核心个人消费支出（PCE）通胀率有所回升
item.description: 1月份美国消费者支出增幅略高于预期，加之核心通胀持续走强以及中东战事拖延，这些因素共同强化了经济学家的观点，即美联储在相当长一段时间内不会重启降息。【原文：U.S. consumer spending increased slightly more than expected in January, which together with continued strength in underlying inflation and the dragging war in the Middle East bolstered economists' views that the Federal ​Reserve would not resume cutting interest rates for some time.】
